completed_tasks.progress: Fix applied to STM stm_window_data.gd: removed count-based fallback from get_demand/get_count_demand. ZIP rebuilt (18745 bytes) and installed. Awaiting in-game verification.

blockers.description: Graph mode fix needs in-game verification — user relaunched game with new ZIP but hasn't confirmed graph mode behavior yet

human_actions_pending.action: Relaunch Upload Labs. Connect STM to 2 Thread Managers. Switch modes: (1) Demand shows real Hz demand, (2) Ratio shows real Hz demand, (3) Graph shows real Hz demand (NOT 0Hz), (4) switch back from Graph to Demand — demand stays real Hz, not drops to 0
human_actions_pending.context: Fix applied: removed count-based demand fallback in stm_window_data.gd. count fallback created feedback loop: Graph mode reduced allocation -> lower count -> lower demand -> less allocation. Now falls back to 1.0 constant instead.

decisions.decision: Removed count-based demand fallback from get_demand() and get_count_demand() in stm_window_data.gd
decisions.rationale: count as demand proxy creates feedback loop in Graph mode: Graph reduces allocation -> count drops -> demand drops -> even less allocation -> stabilizes at ~0.25Hz. Constant 1.0 fallback is correct for windows without required property.

decisions.decision: ZIP packaging base must be MOD DIRECTORY (Smrt Thread Manager/), NOT mods-unpacked/ subdirectory
decisions.rationale: Mod loader requires entries prefixed with mods-unpacked/kuuk-SmartThreadManager/. Wrong base produces entries without that prefix, silently breaking mod loading.

decisions.decision: state.demand = value when no consumers (storage-only case)
decisions.rationale: Thread Manager windows are STM_STORAGE with no demand. distribution_ratio was setting state.demand=0, making progress bar 0%. Fix: use incoming resource value as demand when total=0.

decisions.decision: connection_out_set signal + tick fallback (preserve, never remove)
decisions.rationale: Core fix for update_connections() not firing. In both smart_resource_container.gd files.

decisions.decision: [STM-DIAG] prints in godot.log come from Upload Labs.exe binary, NOT our mod
decisions.rationale: grep -r DIAG game dir matched binary. Our installed ZIP confirmed clean (no DIAG string in any entry). Game has its own diagnostic system using same prefix.

next_action: Ask user: did graph mode show real demand (not 0Hz) after relaunch? Did Demand mode stay stable after switching back from Graph? If yes, commit, clean up print, repackage, run 14-point verification.
context_notes: STM fix NOT committed yet. Progress bar confirmed working (Images 1-4 show non-zero). Graph mode bug identified as count-based demand feedback loop — fix applied. [STM-DIAG] in log = game binary, ignore. SGM uses different architecture (goal-based demand) and does NOT have this feedback loop issue. smart_resource_container.gd working tree has blank lines added + rc.id shim improvement — these are minor, also uncommitted.
